_note: system-decompose manifest for the whole-fleet catalog scope change (ADR-0087, a2ui-catalog.spec.md §5.2/N2/R3). OUTSIDE-IN: the catalog subsystem divides into the fleet-derived coverage gate (PREP) + one executable row-unit per uncatalogued shipped control + the final drain. INSIDE-OUT: the capabilities are 'derive/subtract/assert/bite' (the gate) and per-type 'expose as agent-emittable type / resolve composite adjacency / wire the value seam / prove conformance / drain the allowlist'. Every leaf is one a2ui-builder dispatch. Edges: the gate lands FIRST (seeded allowlist keeps it green), the drain lands LAST (reconciles the residue).

nodes.label: PREP: fleet-derived coverage gate + seeded exclusion allowlist (rewrite default/index.test.ts)
nodes.accept: `npm test -- catalog/default/index.test.ts` green: the derive(descriptor-glob→PascalCase) / subtract(allowlist) / assert(expected ⊆ catalog ∧ factory-present) blocks pass with the allowlist seeded to every not-yet-catalogued fleet type; the synthetic negative control (`typesMissingCatalog(['ZzFake'], catalogKeys, allowlist)` non-empty) proves the gate BITES.

nodes.label: Wave A: Icon → ui-icon (display leaf: name + label)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; `validateA2ui({component:'Icon', name:'star', label:'Favourite'})` = {valid:true,failures:[]}; an unknown-prop Icon returns CATALOG; `Icon` removed from the allowlist; SPEC §5.2 gains the Icon row.

nodes.label: Wave A: Menu → ui-menu (open two-way + placement) + MenuItem sanctioned primitive
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; a Menu(open,placement)+MenuItem-children payload validates 0-CATALOG; a bad MenuItem prop returns CATALOG; `Menu` (+ `MenuItem` if adopted) drained from the allowlist; SPEC §5.2 rows added.

nodes.label: Wave A: Popover → ui-popover (open two-way + placement) + trigger/content model
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; a Popover payload with the ratified trigger/content child model validates 0-CATALOG; a negative control fails CATALOG; `Popover` drained; SPEC §5.2 row + the slot-model note added.

nodes.label: Wave A: Tooltip → ui-tooltip (open two-way + placement + delay) + trigger/content model
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; a Tooltip payload validates 0-CATALOG; a negative control fails CATALOG; `Tooltip` drained; SPEC §5.2 row added.

nodes.label: Wave B: RadioGroup → ui-radio-group + Radio child sub-type (Select/Option mirror)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; a RadioGroup(value,name,required)+Radio-children payload round-trips the selected value via the group's commit event (verified against radio-group.ts) 0-CATALOG; a bad Radio prop fails CATALOG; `RadioGroup`+`Radio` drained; SPEC §5.2 rows added.

nodes.label: Wave B: Slider → ui-slider (value/min/max/step two-way on change)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; a Slider(value,min,max,step) payload validates 0-CATALOG and round-trips a numeric value via `change`; a negative control fails; `Slider` drained; SPEC §5.2 row added.

nodes.label: Wave B: SliderMulti → ui-slider-multi (valueLo/valueHi; dual-value seam limitation surfaced)
nodes.accept: Fork C RESOLVED (Kim, 2026-07-06): two types. `npm run check && npm test` green; a SliderMulti(min,max,step,valueLo,valueHi) payload validates 0-CATALOG; the dual-value one-way binding is proven and the absent single `value:{prop,event}` two-way mark is documented (the seam limitation); `SliderMulti` drained from the allowlist.

nodes.label: Wave B: Calendar → ui-calendar (ISO value two-way on change + min/max/name/required/disabled)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; a Calendar(value,min,max) payload validates 0-CATALOG and round-trips the ISO value via `change`; a negative control fails; `Calendar` drained; SPEC §5.2 row added.

nodes.label: Wave B: ComboBox → ui-combo-box (value two-way on change + Option children + strict/label/placeholder)
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green; a ComboBox(value,strict,placeholder)+Option-children payload validates 0-CATALOG and round-trips the committed value via the ratified two-way slot (value-vs-open fork resolved); a negative control fails; `ComboBox` drained; SPEC §5.2 row added.

nodes.label: Wave C: List → ui-list (surface + flex grammar, list semantics) — Fork A RESOLVED INCLUDE
nodes.accept: Fork A RESOLVED (Kim, 2026-07-06): INCLUDE, conditional on usage guidance. `npm run check && npm test` green; a List(elevation,align,gap)+ChildList payload validates 0-CATALOG; `List` drained + SPEC §5.2 row added WITH the Row/Column-vs-List-vs-Grid usage guidance in the Notes column (non-negotiable per Kim's condition) + ADR-0016 List/Grid exclusion superseded.

nodes.label: Wave C: Grid → ui-grid (surface + gap + min, auto-fit track) — Fork A RESOLVED INCLUDE
nodes.accept: Fork A RESOLVED (Kim, 2026-07-06): INCLUDE, conditional on usage guidance. `npm run check && npm test` green; a Grid(elevation,gap,min)+ChildList payload validates 0-CATALOG; `Grid` drained + SPEC §5.2 row added WITH the same required usage guidance.

nodes.label: Wave D: reconcile the allowlist to the (now-empty) fork-deferred residue + flip the gate to full bite
nodes.accept: `npm test -- catalog/default/index.test.ts` green with the exclusion allowlist reduced to EXACTLY Kim's fork-deferred residue — confirmed EMPTY (all four forks resolved INCLUDE, 2026-07-06) except the documentary-only Image/Video rows (no shipped control, never code-derived); the fleet-gate now fails if any non-residue shipped control lacks a catalog type; the index.test.ts frozen key-list is deleted (superseded by the derived assertion).

actions.label: derive the expected catalog type-set from the descriptor glob (controls/*/*.md tag → PascalCase)
subtract the explicit, reasoned exclusion allowlist from the expected set
assert every expected type is present in catalog.json AND has a registered factory
prove the gate bites — a synthetic uncatalogued shipped control fails the assertion
seed the allowlist with every not-yet-catalogued fleet type so the PREP gate lands green
expose Icon as an agent-emittable catalog type (name + label, display leaf, no value/children)
prove an Icon payload validates 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain Icon from the exclusion allowlist + add its SPEC §5.2 row
expose Menu as a catalog type (open two-way toggle + placement enum)
resolve the MenuItem composite adjacency as a sanctioned primitive (the Option precedent)
prove a Menu+MenuItem payload validates 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain Menu/MenuItem from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 rows
expose Popover as a catalog type (open two-way toggle + placement)
resolve the trigger/content child model for the anchored overlay (named-region vs positional)
prove a Popover payload validates 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain Popover from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 row
expose Tooltip as a catalog type (open two-way + placement + delay)
resolve the Tooltip trigger/content child model
prove a Tooltip payload validates 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain Tooltip from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 row
expose RadioGroup as a catalog type (name/disabled/required/variant/orientation + bindable value)
resolve the Radio child sub-type adjacency (value key + label + checked) mirroring Select/Option
verify and wire the RadioGroup value two-way seam against radio-group.ts (accessor + commit event)
prove a RadioGroup+Radio payload round-trips the selected value 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain RadioGroup/Radio from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 rows
expose Slider as a catalog type (value/min/max/step, two-way on change)
prove a Slider payload validates 0-CATALOG, round-trips a numeric value, and a negative control fails
drain Slider from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 row
expose SliderMulti as a catalog type (min/max/step + valueLo/valueHi)
surface the dual-value seam limitation — no single value:{prop,event} two-way mark (fork residue if folded into Slider)
prove a SliderMulti payload validates 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain SliderMulti from the allowlist OR retain with a recorded fold reason
expose Calendar as a catalog type (ISO value two-way on change + min/max/name/required/disabled)
prove a Calendar payload round-trips the ISO value 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain Calendar from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 row
expose ComboBox as a catalog type (value/label/placeholder/strict/name/disabled + Option children)
resolve the two-way slot fork (value vs open) — recommend value (the form value)
prove a ComboBox+Option payload round-trips the committed value 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain ComboBox from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 row
expose List as a catalog type (surface axes + flex grammar + list semantics) if the fork includes
relate List to the Row/Column faithful-flex model and supersede the ADR-0016 List/Grid non-catalog exclusion
prove a List payload validates 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain List from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 row, or keep it listed with the ADR-0016 reason
expose Grid as a catalog type (surface + gap + min, auto-fit track) if the fork includes
prove a Grid payload validates 0-CATALOG and a negative control fails
drain Grid from the allowlist + add SPEC §5.2 row, or keep it listed with the ADR-0016 reason
flip the fleet-gate to full bite (delete the frozen key-list; assert against the derived set minus the residue)
reconcile the allowlist to exactly the fork-deferred residue, each entry carrying a reason + citation
final SPEC §5.2 / LLD-C4/C5 coherence pass — no stale frozen list, no orphan row

the final reconciliation asserts the residue only after Icon has drained

reconciliation runs only after Popover drains
reconciliation runs only after Tooltip drains
reconciliation runs only after RadioGroup/Radio drain
reconciliation runs only after Slider drains
reconciliation runs only after SliderMulti drains or is recorded as fork residue
reconciliation runs only after Calendar drains
